steps.content: Starting again with a Random Forest baseline (100 trees, depth 5) as a broadly reasonable first guess.
Cross-validated accuracy is about 0.960, a solid baseline to try to beat.
Exploring a different model family, Gradient Boosting, rather than immediately refining the Random Forest, in keeping with an explore-first strategy.
Gradient Boosting comes in at about 0.950, roughly a full point below the Random Forest baseline, so this direction does not pay off.

steps.final_recommendation: random_forest, n_estimators=100, max_depth=5 (cv_mean~0.960). As before, the breadth-first exploratory step itself did not improve on the baseline, which ended the search early under the adaptive stopping rule.
